one_time_description: Potential funding recovery of $11,789,992 identified in 2024/25 recipient audit of federal transfer payments for 2021/22 and 2022/23; no recovery initiated as of preparation date
economic_notes: Nation is economically dependent on Government of Canada. Government business enterprises include Mosakahiken Cree Nation Economic Development Corporation, Aseneskak Casino Limited Partnership (1/7 interest), Ininew Project Management, Mahihkan Bus Lines Limited Partnership (1/6 interest), 4915110 Manitoba Limited, MCN Business Centre LP, MCN C-Store & Fuel LP, MCN Cafe LP. Section 95 Housing Project Phase 8 and Healing Center Project under construction.
notes: Disclaimer of Opinion issued by Baker Tilly HMA LLP due to significant weaknesses in internal controls across cash, accounts receivable, inventory, long-term investments, tangible capital assets, accounts payable, contingencies, payroll, revenue/deferred revenue, expenditures, and capital projects. Auditor's report dated April 22, 2025 for fiscal year ended March 31, 2022 - over three years late. Potential funding recovery of $11,789,992 identified in 2024/25 recipient audit. Additional ISC recoverable amount of $470,277 from 2001 and earlier under negotiation. Repayable CMHC advance of $1,666,609 for Section 95 housing project not yet reflected in long-term debt as project incomplete. Community Services segment expenses of $1,347,500 includes housing-related activity. Net debt position of $5,753,549. Eight entities accounted for on modified equity basis including casino and bus line partnerships.
